questions.text: Plaques on teeth are made up of
options: Food particles plus saliva plus mouth acids
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Food particles plus bacteria**

Dental plaque is a sticky microbial biofilm that forms on teeth surfaces.

Option Analysis:
• Food particles only → Incorrect; microorganisms are essential components.
• Food particles plus saliva → Saliva contributes but plaque primarily involves bacteria.
• Food particles + saliva + mouth acids → Acids are produced later by bacteria.
• Food particles + bacteria → Correct.

Exam Insight:
Bacteria convert sugars into acids causing **dental caries**.

questions.text: The normal temperature of human body is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: 37°C**

Normal human body temperature is **37°C (98.6°F)**.

Option Analysis:
• 35°C → Hypothermia level.
• 36°C → Slightly low.
• 37°C → Correct physiological norm.
• 38°C → Fever.

questions.text: Which gland secretes insulin?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Pancreas**

Insulin is secreted by **beta cells of the Islets of Langerhans**.

Option Analysis:
• Pituitary → Master endocrine gland.
• Pancreas → Correct.
• Adrenal → Produces adrenaline.
• Thyroid → Produces thyroxine.

questions.text: Which organ purifies blood in the human body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Kidney**

Kidneys filter metabolic wastes from blood and produce urine.

questions.text: Which organ produces bile in human body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Liver**

The liver produces bile which helps emulsify fats.

questions.text: How many pairs of ribs are there in human body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: 12 pairs**

Human rib cage contains **12 pairs of ribs (24 ribs)**.

questions.text: Which salt is present in maximum quantity in bones?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Calcium phosphate**

Bones contain **hydroxyapatite crystals (calcium phosphate)**.

questions.text: The smallest bone in human body is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Stapes**

Located in the **middle ear**, the stapes is the smallest bone.

questions.text: The smallest bone of human body is located in
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Ear**

Ear ossicles include **malleus, incus, and stapes**.

questions.text: The number of bones in human skull is
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: 28**

The skull includes **cranial bones and facial bones**.

questions.text: Which part of brain controls body balance?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Cerebellum**

The cerebellum controls **coordination, posture and balance**.

questions.text: Which vitamin helps blood clotting?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Vitamin K**

Vitamin K is required for **prothrombin synthesis**.

questions.text: Which organ pumps blood through body?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Heart**

The heart functions as the **circulatory pump**.

questions.text: Which blood cells fight infection?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: WBC**

White blood cells provide **immune defense**.

questions.text: Which pigment gives red colour to blood?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Haemoglobin**

Haemoglobin carries oxygen.

questions.text: Which part of digestive system absorbs most nutrients?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Small intestine**

Villi increase absorption area.

questions.text: Which organ stores bile?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Gall bladder**

Bile is stored and concentrated here.

questions.text: Which part of blood helps clotting?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Platelets**

Platelets trigger **coagulation cascade**.

questions.text: Which gas is released during respiration?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Carbon dioxide**

Produced during **cellular respiration**.

questions.text: Which organ produces urine?
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Kidney**

Urine formation occurs in **nephrons**.
